title: Add JSON schema for Helm chart values
summary: Adds a JSON Schema Draft-07 (values.schema.json) for the keystone-operator Helm chart that validates all values.yaml fields with correct types, constraints, descriptions, and defaults. Includes 25 helm-unittest test cases covering type constraints, enum validation, min/max boundaries, additionalProperties rejection, and resource quantity pattern validation. Chart.yaml is annotated with ArtifactHub metadata. Reference documentation is comprehensive and accurate.

tests_checklist.item: Tests exist and pass (100/100 helm-unittest, including 25 new schema tests)
Type constraint edge cases covered (all 10 property types)
Resource quantity pattern rejection tested (string and numeric)
Happy path covered (implicitly via existing deployment_test.yaml with schema active)
Existing tests unaffected (all 75 pre-existing tests still pass)

code_quality_checklist.item: Follows existing Helm test file conventions (# CC-NNNN header, suite, templates)
Schema is minimal and focused (171 lines, no over-engineering)
All schema defaults match values.yaml defaults exactly
No dead code or unused properties

security_checklist.item: No secrets or sensitive data in schema or tests
additionalProperties: false prevents injection of unexpected config keys
Input validation enforced at Helm boundary (types, ranges, enums)

architecture_checklist.item: Solution is as simple as possible (single schema file, standard JSON Schema Draft-07)
No scope creep beyond requested changes
Chart.yaml annotations are informational only (ArtifactHub), no functional coupling
Reference doc correctly references packaging doc for chart context

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated definitions (resourceQuantity is a shared $ref)
No speculative properties or constraints beyond values.yaml
No unnecessary schema features (no if/then, no oneOf beyond resourceQuantity)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Schema validates before template rendering (Helm native behavior)
Error messages include JSON path to offending value

defensive_checklist.item: All external inputs validated (every values.yaml field has type + constraints)
Resource quantities validated via pattern + numeric minimum

issues_found.description: Missing additionalProperties rejection tests for rbac, webhook, and resources.requests objects. The pattern is tested on 7/10 object levels (root, image, resources, resources.limits, metrics, leaderElection, serviceAccount) but not on rbac, webhook, or resources.requests. While the schema correctly sets additionalProperties: false on all objects and would reject invalid input, the test coverage is incomplete for these 3 objects.
issues_found.fix: Add 3 tests under the REQ-008 section: (1) should reject unknown property in rbac (set rbac.extraField: true), (2) should reject unknown property in webhook (set webhook.port: 9443), (3) should reject unknown property in resources.requests (set resources.requests.gpu: '1').
issues_found.evidence: Objects tested: root (line 127), image (line 134), resources (line 142), resources.limits (line 150), metrics (line 159), leaderElection (line 167), serviceAccount (line 175). Missing: rbac, webhook, resources.requests.

issues_found.description: The existing packaging reference doc directory layout tree does not include values.schema.json. After this feature, the tree is incomplete. The file docs/reference/backend/keystone-operator-packaging.md shows the helm chart directory structure but omits the new values.schema.json file.
issues_found.fix: Add 'values.schema.json' line to the directory tree between values.yaml and crds/ with description 'JSON Schema for values validation (CC-0069)'.
issues_found.evidence: Directory tree shows Chart.yaml, values.yaml, crds/, templates/ but not values.schema.json. The file now exists at operators/keystone/helm/keystone-operator/values.schema.json.

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a comment in values.yaml referencing the schema file for discoverability (e.g., '# Validated by values.schema.json (CC-0069)').

The resourceQuantity pattern does not cover Kubernetes exponential notation (e.g., '1e3') or leading-dot decimals (e.g., '.5'). These are rare in practice but technically valid K8s resource quantities. Current coverage handles all common formats per REQ-009.
